title: Superman Soars onto the Comic Scene
story: Faster than a speeding bullet! On April 18, 1938, a hero in a red cape burst onto the pages of Action Comics #1. Superman, the Man of Steel, made his grand debut. Little did anyone know that this character would fly into the hearts of millions and become an enduring pop culture icon.
activity_question: What was the name of the comic book where Superman first appeared?

title: Giant Snake Fossil Slithers into History
story: Scientists made a jaw-dropping discovery in 2024! In a mine in western India, they unearthed fossil evidence of the largest known snake ever. title: New Worlds Beyond Our Solar System
story: Astronomers made an exciting discovery in 2013! On April 18, they found two planets that might be a bit like Earth, orbiting a faraway star called Kepler-62. These planets are in the star's 'Goldilocks zone' - not too hot, not too cold. This means they could possibly have liquid water, which is important for life as we know it. The find sparked new hope in the search for worlds beyond our solar system.

title: Midnight Riders Spread the Alarm
story: Hoofbeats echoed through the night as Paul Revere and his fellow riders galloped across Massachusetts. On April 18, 1775, Revere, William Dawes, and Samuel Prescott raced from Charlestown to Lexington. Their urgent mission? To warn colonists that British troops were on the move. As they rode, they shouted, 'The regulars are coming out!' This daring ride helped spark the American Revolution.

title: China's Sinking Cities: A Growing Concern
story: Alarming news emerged on April 18, 2024. A fresh report revealed that almost half of China's cities are slowly sinking into the ground! This isn't because of magic or monsters, but due to two main reasons. First, rapid building and development are putting too much weight on the land. Second, people are pumping out way too much water from underground. These actions are causing the earth beneath many Chinese cities to slowly compress and sink.
activity_question: What are the two main reasons Chinese cities are sinking?
activity_choices: Rapid building and extracting too much water

activity_answer: Rapid building and extracting too much water

title: Poland's First King Takes the Crown
story: A new era dawned for Poland in 1025. Bolesław I, known as 'the Brave', became the country's very first king. On April 18, in the city of Gniezno, he was crowned in a grand ceremony. This moment marked a big step for Poland, turning it from a duchy into a proper kingdom. Bolesław's bravery and leadership had earned him this historic honor.
activity_question: What was Bolesław I's nickname?
follow_up_question: Why do you think it was important for Poland to have its first king?

title: Cannons Roar: The Battle for New Orleans
story: Boom! Cannons thundered as Union ships faced Confederate forts in 1862. This fierce battle near New Orleans began on April 18, with Union forces attacking Forts Jackson and St. Philip. The clash was part of a plan to capture the important Southern port city. After days of intense fighting, the Union Navy broke through, leading to New Orleans' surrender.
activity_question: What were the two Confederate forts attacked by Union forces?
follow_up_question: Why was capturing New Orleans important for the Union?
